_meta.notes: First CMA bundle for Sonuswealth. Sibling to UK-master-2026.1.1.json (tax bundle); no field overlap. Per CF v1.5 §7.3 patch (s17b-1c-cma close), the 5 retail-reference fields (tvLicenceFee, hmrcInterestRate, nhsPrescriptionCharges, childcareFreeHours, taxFreeChildcareMax-equivalent) move from claimed-tax-bundle ownership to CMA bundle ownership. All monetary values in GBP. All rates as decimals. Time-sensitive fields (cashRate, hmrcInterestRate, giltYields, tvLicenceFee) carry snapshot dates in their _source; verify against publisher at next refresh.

sourceUniverseDecisions.O-CF-RULES-01_assetUniverse: B · Standard 8 (UK eq · Global eq DM ex-UK · EM eq · UK gilts short · UK gilts medium · UK corp credit · Property/REIT · Cash)
sourceUniverseDecisions.O-CF-RULES-05_swrMorningstar: B · Localise to UK (UK-grounded SWR derivation; closes O-CF-RULES-02 in same step)
sourceUniverseDecisions.O-CF-RULES-10_expenseBands: C · Persona-aligned 5 cuts (Foundation 18-30 · Accumulation 30-45 · Pre-retirement 45-60 · Transition 60-67 · Decumulation 67+) × 3 PLSA tiers × essential/discretionary
sourceUniverseDecisions.O-CF-RULES-12_coiDiscountRate: B · Risk-free interim (UK 10-yr gilt yield from cma.giltYields; PCC upgrade hook reserved)
sourceUniverseDecisions.Finding1_retailReferenceFieldOwnership: CMA bundle owns 5 fields previously claimed by CF §7.3 tax bundle. CF v1.5 → v1.6 patch noted at session close.

inflationPath._source: OBR Economic and Fiscal Outlook March 2026 — central CPI forecast 2.3% for 2026 (OBR Mar 2026 EFO §1.14)
OBR Economic and Fiscal Outlook March 2026 — central CPI forecast 2.0% from 2027 onward; aligns with BoE 2% target
OBR Economic and Fiscal Outlook March 2026 — long-run target convergence
BoE 2% inflation target (long-run anchor; OBR EFO converges)
BoE 2% inflation target (years 6+ revert per CMA convention)

_inflationPathNotes: Years 1-2 from OBR EFO Mar 2026 published forecast. Years 3-5 OBR forecast extension converging to 2% target. Years 6+ revert to BoE inflation target per standard CMA convention (terminal value assumption). Refresh annually around 6 April with new OBR EFO.

uk._source: Vanguard VCMM 10-year UK equity nominal return projection (mean ~5.8%) · long-run UK equity volatility ~15.5% from Dimson-Marsh-Staunton Yearbook 2024 (UK 1900-2023 series). Conservative: mean is mid-range of major asset manager CMAs (Vanguard 5.5-6.0%, Schroders 5.5-7.0%, Morningstar 5.8-6.2%).

global._source: MSCI World ex-UK 10-year nominal return projection ~6.2% (Vanguard VCMM 2025 + Schroders CMA 2025 mid-range) · vol ~14.5% from MSCI World long-run series.

emerging._source: MSCI EM 10-year nominal return projection ~7.2% (Vanguard VCMM 2025 EM range 6.8-7.5%) · vol ~21.5% from MSCI EM long-run series. Higher mean reflects EM risk premium; higher vol reflects EM volatility characteristic.

short._source: UK gilts short-duration (3-yr) 10-year nominal expected return ~3.8% (Vanguard VCMM 2025) · vol ~3.0% from FTSE Actuaries UK Gilts Short Index long-run series.

medium._source: UK gilts medium-duration (7-yr) 10-year nominal expected return ~4.2% (Vanguard VCMM 2025) · vol ~6.2% from FTSE Actuaries UK Gilts All-Stocks long-run series.

corporateCredit._source: UK corporate credit (investment-grade, 7-yr avg duration) 10-year expected return ~4.8% (Schroders CMA 2025) · vol ~7.5% from iBoxx Sterling Corporate Index long-run series. Spread over gilts ~80-100bps.

giltYields._source: BoE yield curve · 1-yr nominal · approximate snapshot from BoE published yield curve data; refresh from https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/statistics/yield-curves at bundle activation
BoE yield curve · 3-yr nominal · approximate snapshot
BoE yield curve · 5-yr nominal · approximate snapshot
BoE yield curve · 10-yr nominal · approximate snapshot · used as risk-free rate for CF CoI variant NPV per O-CF-RULES-12 = B (Risk-free interim)
BoE yield curve · 15-yr nominal · approximate snapshot
BoE yield curve · 20-yr nominal · approximate snapshot
BoE yield curve · 25-yr nominal · approximate snapshot
BoE yield curve · 30-yr nominal · approximate snapshot

_giltYieldsNotes: Yield curve snapshot 2026-05-08. Daily BoE publication; bundle is point-in-time snapshot. Refresh at each bundle effective period; CF Floor-Upside (§3.4) consumes for bond ladder pricing.

annuityRates._source: William Burrows Annuities published quarterly tables · CMI SAPS S3 mortality base · level annuity, no guarantee
William Burrows · age 65 male — most-quoted reference rate

_annuityRatesNotes: 40 rows: 5 ages (55/60/65/70/75) × 2 sexes (M/F) × 4 (guarantee × escalation) combinations. 0% escalation = level annuity. 3% escalation = annual fixed escalation (RPI-equivalent). 5/10-yr guarantee = continued payment to dependant if annuitant dies within guarantee period. Rates are gross of any healthcare/lifestyle uplifts. Snapshot 2026-Q1 from William Burrows quarterly publication. Refresh quarterly. Conservative — actual market rates vary ±5% by provider and underwriting.

propertyGrowthRate._source: ONS UK House Price Index long-run trend · Nationwide HPI 1975-2024 series · real (CPI-adjusted) annual growth ~1.0-1.5%. Mid-point 1.2% used. Consistent with Land Registry long-run series.

cashRate._source: BoE Bank Rate held at 3.75% on 30 April 2026 MPC (8-1 vote, 1 dissent for 4.0%). Used as conservative cash anchor. Retail savings rates vary ±50bps from base. Source: BoE Monetary Policy Summary 30 April 2026.

correlationMatrix._source: Asset-class correlations derived from: Vanguard VCMM 2025 correlation matrix · Dimson-Marsh-Staunton Yearbook 2024 long-run UK series · Brealey-Myers-Allen Principles of Corporate Finance Ch.10 (2024 ed). 8x8 universe per O-CF-RULES-01 = B (Standard 8). Symmetric, diagonal=1.0, all values ∈ [-1,1]. Positive-semi-definite verified at session close.

gkDefaults._source: Guyton (2004) 'Decision Rules and Maximum Initial Withdrawal Rates' Journal of Financial Planning · Guyton & Klinger (2006) 'Decision Rules and Portfolio Management for Retirees' Journal of Financial Planning. Trigger ±20%, adjustment ±10% per papers. Inflation cap and negative-year freeze are standard G-K modifiers.

bengen._source: Bengen (1994) 'Determining Withdrawal Rates Using Historical Data' Journal of Financial Planning. US-anchored; 30-yr horizon, 50/50 stocks/bonds. Used as upper-bound conservative reference.

morningstarUKAdjusted._source: Morningstar 2024 retirement income research (US baseline 3.7% with 30-yr horizon). UK localisation per O-CF-RULES-05 = B: applies same Morningstar methodology to UK return assumptions in this CMA bundle (uk_equity 5.8% · uk_gilts_medium 4.2% · CPI 2.0%) yielding ~3.4% UK-grounded SWR. Closes O-CF-RULES-02 (G-K UK initial rate calibration) — same UK-grounded SWR is the G-K starting rate.
morningstarUKAdjusted._localisationNote: Morningstar US 3.7% adopts lower equity premium and tighter inflation expectations. UK derivation applies same horizon (30-yr), same success threshold (90%), same withdrawal pattern (real-terms constant) to UK CMA inputs. Result: 3.4% reflects UK gilt premium and UK equity volatility characteristics.

guytonKlinger._source: Guyton & Klinger (2006) per gkDefaults source. UK initial rate 5.4% reflects flexibility allowance from G-K adjustment rules vs constant-real Bengen/Morningstar approach. Localised to UK CMA per O-CF-RULES-02 closure.

vanguardDynamic._source: Vanguard 'A more dynamic approach to spending for investors in retirement' research methodology. Rate-of-return-adjusted ceilings and floors.

expenseBands._structure: 5 life stages (foundation_18_30 · accumulation_30_45 · pre_retirement_45_60 · transition_60_67 · decumulation_67_plus) × 3 PLSA tiers (minimum · moderate · comfortable) × essential vs discretionary split. Per O-CF-RULES-10 = C (Persona-aligned).

minimum._source: ONS Family Spending Survey 2022/23 · single-person under-30 essentials baseline · scaled to 2026 prices via CPI

moderate._source: ONS FSS 2022/23 mid-quintile single-person under-30 · CPI-adjusted

comfortable._source: ONS FSS 2022/23 upper-quintile single-person under-30 · CPI-adjusted

minimum._source: PLSA Retirement Living Standards 2024 (single, transition adapted from pre-retirement single Minimum tier) · CPI-adjusted to 2026

moderate._source: PLSA RLS 2024 (single Moderate, transition phase) · CPI-adjusted

comfortable._source: PLSA RLS 2024 (single Comfortable, transition phase) · CPI-adjusted

minimum._source: PLSA Retirement Living Standards 2024 single Minimum (£14,400 incl basic essentials) · CPI-adjusted to 2026

moderate._source: PLSA RLS 2024 single Moderate (£31,300 total split essential/discretionary at typical 80/20) · CPI-adjusted

comfortable._source: PLSA RLS 2024 single Comfortable (£43,100 total split 75/25) · CPI-adjusted

_expenseBandsNotes: Working-age (foundation/accumulation/pre-retirement) cuts derived from ONS Family Spending Survey 2022/23, CPI-adjusted to 2026 prices. Retirement (transition/decumulation) cuts derived from PLSA Retirement Living Standards 2024 (couple figures available; single-person used as conservative anchor for SWR derivation). Essential = housing + utilities + food + transport + basic clothing + insurance. Discretionary = leisure + holidays + dining + non-essential goods. Tier breakpoints align with PLSA Minimum/Moderate/Comfortable definitions where retirement tiers apply.

hmrcInterestRate.formula: BoE base + 4% (late payment, post-6 April 2025) · BoE base - 1% with 0.5% floor (repayment)
hmrcInterestRate._source: HMRC published rates: late payment 7.75% from 9 January 2026 · repayment 2.75% from 9 January 2026. Both reflect BoE base rate at 3.75%. Source: gov.uk/government/publications/rates-and-allowances-hmrc-interest-rates
hmrcInterestRate._refreshTrigger: BoE base rate change; auto-update within ~3 weeks of MPC announcement

tvLicenceFee._source: TV Licensing standard colour TV fee April 2025 - March 2026 = £174.50 (April 2025 uplift from £169.50; CPI-linked annual review). April 2026 uplift not yet confirmed at bundle effective date; verify and patch at activation if announced.

vehicleExciseDuty._structure: Standard rates (post-first-year) for petrol/diesel cars registered from April 2017. First-year rates and supplementary tables truncated for v1; expand at next bundle if needed.
vehicleExciseDuty._evChangeNote: EVs subject to standard VED from 1 April 2025; previously exempt.
vehicleExciseDuty._source: gov.uk Vehicle Excise Duty rates 2025-26 · April 2026 uplift CPI-linked; verify at activation

taxFreeChildcareExtended._source: gov.uk Tax-Free Childcare and Childcare Choices · extended free hours rollout from September 2024 · TFC max £2,000/year per child (£500/quarter) at 20% government top-up of parent contributions. Per O-CF-RULES-11.

childcareFreeHours._source: gov.uk universal 15 free hours childcare for all 3-4 year olds (separate from extended/working-parent entitlement). Finding 1 addition; missing from prep §4.

nhsPrescriptionCharges._source: NHS Business Services Authority published prescription charges 2025-26 (England-only; abolished in Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland). Finding 1 addition; missing from prep §4.

independentSchoolVAT._source: Removal of VAT exemption on private school fees per Finance Act 2025. Per O-CF-RULES-08; gov.uk policy paper 'Applying VAT to private school fees and removing the business rates charitable rates relief for private schools'.
